response.justification: Bogdan is a genuinely seasoned, polyglot engineer with real developer-tooling credentials — he worked on NI LabWindows/CVI (an actual IDE) and has built test runners, assertion libraries, and web servers from scratch — but the role's core required stack is largely absent. His Java exposure is peripheral (side usage on robotics projects, a skills-list mention), there is no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise-framework experience, and no evidence of deep JVM internals work; his depth lies in TypeScript/Node and DLang instead. The tooling passion is a good signal, but the gap on the explicitly required Java/Kotlin/Spring/JVM expertise is too wide for a mid-senior IC expected to own enterprise-framework subsystems end to end.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI, plus self-driven language-tooling projects (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Proven work on complex, high-scale systems (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic)
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testability focus, and active knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring/enterprise-framework experience, both explicit required qualifications
Java appears only as incidental, dated experience — no evidence of deep JVM understanding
Recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node full-stack, AI tooling) points away from the JVM ecosystem this role centers on

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language-tooling track record
